title: Bridge Essay: My body in the brain: a neurocognitive model of body-ownership.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Birth/Body** (Phase 1) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Birth/Body to mechanistic neuroscience through My, body, in, the, brain:.

**My body in the brain: aneurocognitive model of body-ownership.** — Neuropsychologia. PMID: 19819247. DOI: 10.1016/j.neuropsychologia.2009.09.034. Empirical research on the bodily self has only recently started to investigate how the link between abody and the experience of this body as mine is developed, maintained or disturbed. The Rubber Hand Illusion has been used as amodel instance of the normal sense of embodiment to investigate the processes that underpin the experience of body-ownership. This review puts forward aneurocognitive model according to which body-ownership arises as an interaction between current multisensory input an
